rulings.Case Title: CAITLIN FITTING et al VS. PAUL KRAAIJVANGER et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 12, 2016, line 1. DEFENDANT PAUL KRAAIJVANGER'S MOTION TO TAX COSTS. OFF CALENDAR to be re-filed, is necessary, after appeal is resolved. Plaintiff not to file any further cost bills. This action is stayed and no statutory deadlines for costs and fees shall be applicable during the stay. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: CAITLIN FITTING et al VS. PAUL KRAAIJVANGER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Plaintiffs Motion To Increase Appeal Bond
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 12, 2016, line 1. PLAINTIFF CAITLIN FITTING'S Motion To Increase Appeal Bond. DENIED. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: CAITLIN FITTING et al VS. PAUL KRAAIJVANGER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Plaintiffs Motion For An Order Awarding Reasonable Attorneys Fees Postjudgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 12, 2016, line 1. PLAINTIFF CAITLIN FITTING'S Motion For An Order Awarding Reasonable Attorneys Fees Postjudgment. DENIED as to the claimed amount of $31,590. Moving party failed to show entitlement to such fees per CCP 685.080, 685.040 and 1033.5(10)(A), specifically moving party failed to show that the underlying judgment included an award of attorney's fees authorized by contract. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL ROUPPET VS. FERGUS O'SULLIVAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Third Party Tommy Carty And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 12, 2016, line 2. PLAINTIFF MICHAEL ROUPPET'S Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Third Party Tommy Carty And Request For Monetary Sanctions. OFF CALENDAR. On December 1, 2016 Plaintiff obtained an OST for this motion. OST provided that opposition to the motion was due on December 7, 2016. Plaintiff served the OST, which contained the briefing schedule, on the parties/third party on December 5, 2016 by overnight mail and e-service. Plaintiff additionally failed to provide courtesy copy of the moving papers in violation of SFLR 2.7B. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: BONNIE KNIGHT VS. RAND NATHAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 12, 2016, line 3. Defendant's Motion for Protective Order. GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion is granted as to the Notice of Deposition. Objections to categories 22, 23, 24, 25, 26, 27, 28, 38 and 39 (medical condition) sustained. Motion is otherwise denied. Requests for sanctions are denied. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: BONNIE KNIGHT VS. RAND NATHAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Rand Nathan Defendants Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena For Production Of Business Records Is Continued To November 30, 2016 And Transferred To Department 301 To Be Heard At 9:00 A.M. Motion For Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 12, 2016, line 3. Defendant's Motion to Quash Deposition Subpoena. GRANTED. Sanctions granted against Plaintiff's counsel in the amount of $1,875 to be paid within 10 days of notice of entry of order. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: STEVE RYAN VS. MITCHELL ROSENFELD ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Demmurrer To Plaintiff'S First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 12, 2016, line 4. Demmurrer To Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint. Sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer on res judicata grounds is OVERRULED. Moving party failed to cite any authority supporting it argument that judgment in 2010 case is "final" for the purposes of having a preclusive effect. Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the 1-3 causes of action as they are barred by the Statute of Limitations. Demurrer to the 4-6 causes of action is overruled as these causes of action are not barred by res judicata or statute of limitations; complaint sufficiently alleges that partnership agreements are not written. =(501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL HO VS. CAN TANG YU
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Granting Pltf'S Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, December 12, 2016, line 5. DEFENDANT CAN YU'S Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Granting Plaintiff's Motion For Summary Judgment. Transferred to Department 611 to be heard on December 12, 2016 at 9:00 a.m. Defendant's Motion for Reconsideration is DENIED. Moving party fails to meet the requirements of CCP 1008. See Opposition to Motion for Summary Judgment and Transcript of October 28, 2016 Proceedings. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is denied. =(501/LOT)
